The duration of synchronization depends on the number of pages, which can take up to 5 minutes to complete.

LIST.title: Users
LIST.subtitle: List of all users from the applications Study Monitor and My Monitor

ADD.description_user: To use AdminMonitorSuite (AMS) and My Monitor you need a user
ADD.description_user_ams: In the case of AMS the user authentication is done via User/pass. In the case of MyMonitor the user authentication is done via the official process used in the Portuguese Public Administration and described at https://www.autenticacao.gov.pt.
ADD.title: Create a new user
ADD.subtitle: Create a new use for one og the following applications: Study Monitor or My Monitor
ADD.password_hint: The password should have at least 8 characters, 1 upper case character, 1 lower case character, 1 number and 1 special character
ADD.password_short: The password has less than 8 characters
ADD.password_uppercase: The password does not contain any upper case character
ADD.password_lowercase: The password does not contain any lower case character
ADD.password_number: The password does not contain any number
ADD.password_special: The password does not contain any special character

list.title: Important:
list.item1: The user and all it's data will be deleted
list.item2: If the user belongs to the Study Monitor application, all it's websites and tags will be deleted

LIST.title: Gov Users
LIST.subtitle: List of all users from the applications Study Monitor and My Monitor

ADD.description_usergov: To use MyMonitor you need a user Autenticação.Gov (user.Gov).
ADD.description_usergov1: Users are a kind of account that aggregates websites to be accessed by a group of people.
ADD.description_usergov2: With MyMonitor the process has become individualized since authentication is individual. Thus, it is necessary that each user.Gov accesses their user/account or group of websites of an account.
ADD.title: Create a new user
ADD.subtitle: Create a new use for one og the following applications: Study Monitor or My Monitor

list.title: Important:
list.item1: The user and all it's data will be deleted
list.item2: If the user belongs to the Study Monitor application, all it's websites and tags will be deleted

ADD.title: Create a new category
ADD.description: Create a new category for the applications Study Monitor or My Monitor
ADD.create_button_tooltip: Create a new category for the applications Study Monitor or My Monitor
ADD.description_category: A Category is an aggregator of websites. Categories aim to aggregate websites by classes. Examples of classes: government area of education, government area of economy, central public administration, schools, public institutes, etc. In this way it is possible to organize the visualization of information in AdminMonitorSuite by lists of websites that are, in some way, related and observe global statistics of these aggregations. A category is very similar to the "tag" classifier that is commonly used in blogs.
ADD.note: Note: the Categories will not be published in the Public Observatory. In the Public Observatory the websites are published via the Directories to which they belong. Categories help to produce and organize the Directories.

LIST.export_data_description: To export all data from the Entity to today's date, press the 'Export CSV' button below.
LIST.title: Entities
LIST.subtitle: List of all entities

ADD.description_entity: An Entity is a group of websites that share a common purpose. Entities are used to group websites that are related to a specific topic or organization. Entities are also used to track the performance of a specific website or group of websites.
ADD.title: Create a new entity
ADD.description: Create a new entity for the applications Study Monitor or My Monitor
ADD.short_name_exists: The inserted short name already exists
ADD.re_evaluate_tooltip: Re-evaluate the websites of the entity

ADD.description_website: The website is the most relevant analysis unit of the Observatory. The analysis of accessibility practices is done on the sample of pages that are part of the website. Sites are aggregated into Categories and Directories and belong to Entities.
